notes.summary: Development refers to the improvement in the quality of life of people. It includes better income, education, healthcare, equality, and security. Development is not only about earning more money but also about living a better life.
notes.importance: Understanding development helps students realise that progress includes social and economic improvements, not just income growth.

notes.summary: Different people have different developmental goals based on their needs and background. Some goals may conflict with others, so development must balance various interests.
notes.importance: This concept explains why development planning must consider fairness and equality.

notes.summary: Per capita income is the average income of a country. It is calculated by dividing total income by total population. It is used to compare development between countries.
notes.importance: It helps in comparing living standards but does not show income distribution.

notes.summary: HDI is a measure prepared by UNDP to assess development based on health, education, and income. It gives a broader view of development beyond money.
notes.importance: HDI helps compare countries using multiple development indicators.

notes.summary: Sustainable development means using resources carefully so that future generations can also meet their needs. Overuse of resources can harm the environment and future growth.
notes.importance: It promotes conservation of resources and environmental protection.
